SEQUENCE.COMMENT: csfwping - service for responding to load balancer availability pings
This service is intended to be executed only by the Citrix Netscaler Load Balancer as a way of determining if this specific instance of
the framework is up and operating properly. This service does a simple check for a particular file on the csutil mount point:
If the file exists, the service returns a 200 OK response. If not, or if any other error occurs, a 590 Service Unavailable is returned. The above
file normally should always exist, but during scheduled downtime or maintenance, this file can be renamed by hand, indicating to the load
balancer that client requests should not be routed to this instance. The initial design of this calls for the load balancer to route requests
to all 3 instances of PRODAD1 cluster, and all 3 instances of the PRODAD2 cluster, along with three more standalone instances (PRODFA wm53a:28000,
PRODFB wm53b:28000 and PRODFC wm53c:28000) that will serve as "fallback" instances for handling requests during a maintenance window. These
three instances, under normal conditions, will always be disabled and idle, meaning, its host-port file will be renamed to something other than what is
Note that this service has to be able to execute anonymously - make sure the execute ACL permission is set as such
Note also that this service uses a URL alias - it should be invoked using http://host:port/csfw/ping
